_comment: Please place new simulation entries in alphabetical order. NOTE: This is the per-sim copy for the Planetary Orbit Simulator. The shared foundation/contents.json contains entries for every sim; because the masthead only reads this sim's key, this copy carries just this sim's entry (Help/About content derived verbatim from the original Flash sim's texts) plus the newSim template. See CONVERSION_NOTES.md.

help.title: Help and Instructions
help.content: Leave this string completely blank for no Help button.

about.title: About this Demonstrator
about.content:
For additional astronomy education materials please visit Astronomy Education at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln.

This demonstrator has been modernized by the AAS Applet Task Force to meet modern web accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1 AA).

Initial funding for this work was provided by NSF grants #0231270 and/or #0404988.

Permission is granted to use these files for noncommercial purposes as long as they remain unmodified.

help.title: Help and Instructions
help.content:
This simulator models the motion of a hypothetical planet that orbits the sun according to Kepler's laws of motion.

The size and eccentricity of the planet's orbit can set using the sliders in the Orbit Settings panel. Presets for the existing planets can be loaded by selecting a planet from the combo box and clicking OK.

Since the planets take months to years to orbit the sun the animation is shown speeded up. You can control the animation rate in the Animation Controls panel. The rate is specified in simulated years per second.

The Visualization Options panel provides options for showing the solar system planets and their orbits. One thing to note is that all of the planets' orbits are shown pointing in the same direction. In reality this is not the case (nor are the planets in exactly the same plane).

The clear optional features hides the solar system planets and resets the features selected in the tabbed panel.

The tabbed panel beneath the visualization lets you control options specific to each of Kepler's laws. The Newtonian Features tab lets you see the velocity and acceleration vectors of the planet.

The orbit visualization window is partially interactive. The planet's position in the diagram can be adjusted by dragging it around (or, with the planet focused, using the arrow keys), as can the sweeps created using the Kepler's 2nd Law tab controls.

about.title: About this Simulator
about.content:
This simulator is part of the Planetary Orbit Simulator Module of the Nebraska Astronomy Applet Project. Supporting materials and additional astronomy education resources can be found there.

This simulator has been modernized by the AAS Applet Task Force to meet modern web accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1 AA).

Funding for this work was provided by NSF grants #0231270 and/or #0404988.

Permission is granted to use this file for noncommercial purposes as long as it remains unmodified.
